title: [arbok] Battle Guide
summary: A pure-[poison] physical sweeper that uses [intimidate] on entry and [coil] to snowball its way to a sweep.
strengths: [coil] boosts Attack, Defense, and accuracy in one slot
[sucker-punch] covers the low Speed with priority
weaknesses: Speed 80 loses races before a setup turn is secured
60/69/79 bulk leaves very little setup room

recommendedMoves.reason: [ground] coverage for Steel, Rock, and Electric
Triple boost to Attack, Defense, and accuracy

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[arbok] is a pure-[poison] physical sweeper that builds its plan around [intimidate] on entry and [coil] for setup. 60/69/79 bulk and Speed 80 are plain, but [intimidate] softens incoming physical attackers while [coil] stacks Attack, Defense, and accuracy in one move, letting [arbok] convert a single setup turn into a legitimate sweep attempt.

## Strengths
[intimidate] drops the opposing physical attackers Attack by one stage on switch-in, effectively padding [arbok] bulk without investment. [coil] is the key move — one use turns [arbok] into a 1.5x Attack, 1.5x Defense threat with guaranteed [gunk-shot] accuracy. [sucker-punch] patches the Speed tier by scoring KOs on weakened attackers regardless of Speed, which is how the sweep ends cleanly against fast cleaners.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[ground] and psychic both hit for 2x, and any respectable [earthquake] user threatens [arbok] before it can [coil]. The thin 60/69/79 bulk means it cannot take a boosted neutral hit, and psychic sweepers race past [arbok] Speed to end the setup before it starts. Entering without [intimidate] active (such as from a predicted U-turn) is usually a losing proposition.

## Recommended Moveset
[gunk-shot] is the locked [poison] STAB because the damage ceiling after a [coil] boost is the whole finisher. [earthquake] is the mandatory coverage for Steel, Rock, and Electric walls that would otherwise wall the STAB. [coil] is the setup slot — one use is enough to kick off a sweep attempt, and the accuracy boost removes any [gunk-shot] miss worries. [sucker-punch] is the last slot for priority cleanup against faster threats.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[life-orb] is the default because the 30% damage boost lets [coil]-boosted attacks reach higher OHKO rolls. [focus-sash] is the insurance pick that guarantees one setup turn, and [sitrus-berry] is a rarer mid-game heal for longer setup plans. Ability stays [intimidate] — shed-skin and unnerve are not competitive. Tera [poison] maxes STAB damage while Tera [ground] flips the psychic weakness and boosts [earthquake].

## Teammates & Synergy
[arbok] relies on forced switches to get a free [coil] turn, so pair it with volt-switch and u-turn pivots that create matchup advantages. A Dark partner handles the psychic weakness, and Flying or Grass allies cover the [ground] weakness. A stealth-rock user behind [arbok] widens the OHKO ranges after a [coil] boost, turning 2HKOs into guaranteed KOs.

## Play Patterns
Bring [arbok] in on a predicted switch or a soft matchup, [coil] on the free turn, then start [gunk-shot] + [earthquake] cleanup. Use [sucker-punch] only against weakened attackers, and Tera on a telegraphed psychic or [ground] move to buy the decisive turn.
